title: Lesson Quiz: Taxes and Revenue
description: Assessment of the concept that Government spending and taxation.

choices.text: Money collected by government from individuals or businesses to raise revenue
choices.feedback: Correct. A tax is money collected by government to help fund services and operations.

choices.text: A payment made only between private businesses
choices.feedback: Taxes are collected by government, not just exchanged between private businesses.

choices.text: A reward given to consumers after shopping
choices.feedback: A tax is not a reward or rebate.

choices.text: A law that controls pollution from factories
choices.feedback: That is a regulation, not a tax.

questions.prompt: Income tax is a tax on money earned by individuals or businesses.

choices.feedback: Correct. Income tax is based on earnings.
This is incorrect. Income tax is collected from earned income.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows a sales tax?

choices.text: A homeowner pays a yearly charge based on the value of a house
Part of a worker's paycheck is withheld before take-home pay is received
choices.feedback: That is more closely related to income tax.

choices.text: An added amount appears at checkout when a customer buys a backpack
choices.feedback: Correct. Sales tax is added to many purchases of goods and services.

choices.text: A business pays a fee to advertise a product online
choices.feedback: That is not the best example of a sales tax.

choices.text: To raise revenue for public services and government responsibilities
choices.feedback: Correct. Governments collect taxes to fund services, programs, and operations.

choices.text: To make sure all products cost more every year
choices.feedback: Taxes may affect prices, but that is not their main purpose.

choices.text: To prevent all households from spending money
choices.feedback: Governments do not collect taxes to stop all spending.

choices.text: To remove the need for government services
choices.feedback: Taxes help fund government services rather than remove the need for them.

questions.prompt: Property tax is based on the value of property such as land or buildings.

choices.feedback: Correct. Property tax is based on the value of property.
This is incorrect. Property tax is tied to property value.
